case.title: H. F. C. v. NEW JERSEY TAX DIRECTOR

opinion.h1: H. F. C. v. NEW JERSEY TAX DIRECTOR, 371 U.S. 13 (1962)
opinion.h4: HOUSEHOLD FINANCE CORP. v. DIRECTOR OF THE DIVISION OF TAXATION, DEPARTMENT OF THE TREASURY OF NEW JERSEY. APPEAL FROM THE SUPREME COURT OF NEW JERSEY. No. 237. Decided October 8, 1962.
opinion.p: Arthur J. Sills, Attorney General of New Jersey, and Alan B. Handler, Deputy Attorney General, for appellee.
The motion to dismiss is granted and the appeal is dismissed. Treating the papers whereon the appeal was taken as a petition for writ of certiorari, certiorari is denied.
MR. JUSTICE GOLDBERG took no part in the consideration or decision of this case.
